headline: Iran ceasefire drives broad bullish consensus across indices and mega-cap tech
summary: All five AI models converged on broad S&P 500 strength driven by U.S.-Iran de-escalation optimism, with 4 of 5 predicting SPY gains and strong agreement on tech upside. Claude made the boldest individual call with ARM's 15% pre-market jump on new AGI chip news, while bearish outliers included Claude's APO redemption warning and Grok's contrarian TSLA downside call. Actual market closes remain pending, but pre-market momentum strongly supported the consensus bullish thesis across equity indices.

consensus_picks.note: Four of five models predicted SPY gains with targets ranging 600-666, unified by Iran ceasefire optimism and positive futures momentum.
Tech upside consensus driven by NVDA and TSLA pre-market strength in Nasdaq-100 futures.
Bullish calls from GPT-4o and Perplexity conflicted with Grok's bearish production delay thesis, showing model disagreement.

best_call.summary: Claude's highest-conviction call on ARM's 15% pre-market surge following AGI chip announcement and Raymond James upgrade demonstrated prescient identification of a genuine catalyst with 0.88 confidence.

worst_call.summary: Grok's bearish TSLA call based on X/Twitter production concerns contradicted broader market momentum and conflicted with bullish calls from competing models on the same stock.
